response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions such as hives or anaphylaxis in sensitive individuals  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins  
Peanut proteins can be present in food or other substances even in aquatic environments  
Allergic reactions are not influenced by the presence of water  

Sec. 2  
The body's immune response to allergens is not affected by environmental conditions such as water  
People with peanut allergies must avoid allergens regardless of their surroundings  
Underwater living does not provide a solution to managing peanut allergies  
Allergies require careful management through avoidance and medical preparedness  
The inability to live underwater is unrelated to peanut allergies
